run_summary: Screen 1 faces a basket rule constraint: 10 flags from two AI catalysts (Meta WhatsApp agent + OpenAI Codex), with 4 positions already held from the Codex trigger. All 10 new flags meet BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, confidence ≥3, panic_calibration=unjustified, threat minimal/none). However, the existing portfolio already reflects conviction on the Codex thesis; the new flags are primarily reconfirmations. Position decisions: hold all four existing trades (days_held=0, thesis intact, thesis is sympathy fade in early innings). New decisions: WATCH all 10 flags to avoid over-concentration and to let the Codex thesis play out in the existing positions before adding.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Meta's WhatsApp Business agent targets retail/SMB customer support workflows; Blackbaud's core franchise is donor management, fundraising, and grantmaking for nonprofits with 20+ year donor histories and 3-year subscription terms with ~40% of contracts renewing in 2026. The Meta trigger has no mechanism to displace that workflow, yet BLKB took a sharp drop on no company-specific news.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that nonprofit customers are actually evaluating Meta/WhatsApp-based donor engagement as a replacement, or a Q-over-Q acceleration in churn.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (position opened same or prior session). Reconfirmed by this flag: threat=none, panic=unjustified, confidence=4. Eligible for BUY if exited and re-entered, but basket rule applies (10 flags from 2 catalysts). Defer new entry to avoid concentration; existing position is already a test of the thesis.

entries.name: Varonis Systems, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Varonis is structurally a beneficiary of more enterprise AI deployments — data classification and access governance is the substrate AI agents need. WhatsApp Business agent has no mechanism to displace enterprise data security. Today's drop is pure cyber/SaaS sympathy beta to AI-fear flow.
entries.what_kills: Customer disclosure showing AI displacing data-governance spend rather than augmenting it; a slowdown in SaaS conversion.
entries.reasoning: Threat=none, panic=unjustified, confidence=4 passes BUY eligibility. However, this is part of a 10-flag basket (2 catalysts). Varonis is structurally a beneficiary (data security as AI substrate), not a victim—the sympathy fade is thematic SaaS/cyber beta. With 4 Codex positions already held and 10 total eligible flags, adding new positions now creates concentration on a single macro thesis. WATCH and let existing positions establish a price recovery signal before adding.

entries.name: OneSpan Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: OneSpan's authentication franchise is governed by PSD2/eIDAS/FIDO standards and embedded in bank infrastructure. A WhatsApp customer-service agent has zero mechanism to displace e-signature or hardware-token auth in regulated banking. Drop appears to be cyber/auth sympathy fade.
entries.what_kills: Evidence banks are shifting auth workflows into general-purpose AI/messaging stacks (highly unlikely given regulatory regime).
entries.reasoning: Threat=none, panic=unjustified, confidence=4 passes BUY eligibility. Filings confirm authentication/e-signature moat is orthogonal to WhatsApp customer-service agent. However, basket rule applies (10-flag set). With 4 existing positions, adding a 5th from the same macro theme risks over-concentration. WATCH pending price recovery confirmation or a material catalyst on OneSpan fundamentals.

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: EVH's revenue comes from capitated risk-sharing with payers for oncology/cardiology specialty care — fundamentally a clinical-risk business, not a software wrapper that an AI agent can replace. The Meta WhatsApp agent has no plausible mechanism to displace specialty care management contracts.
entries.what_kills: Real oncology utilization deterioration (which has been a real headwind) or a major customer non-renewal — these are EVH's real risks, not AI agents.
entries.reasoning: Threat=minimal (not 'none'), confidence=3 (threshold). Passes BUY eligibility barely, but filings show real risk exposure (oncology utilization headwinds, customer concentration). Thesis is plausible (specialty care management is clinical, not AI-replaceable), but the conviction bar is lower and real business headwinds exist. Basket rule applies. WATCH and await clearer sympathy-fade recovery on more obvious targets.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Bank loan origination is governed by OCC/FDIC examination requirements, KYC, AML, fair lending rules — none of which Meta's customer-service WhatsApp agent addresses. nCino's filings acknowledge AI competition broadly, but the trigger event is wholly orthogonal to commercial loan origination.
entries.what_kills: Acceleration of bank-built internal LLM solutions or a major customer migration off the platform.
entries.reasoning: Threat=minimal, confidence=3 (threshold). Passes BUY eligibility. Thesis is sound (bank loan origination embedded in OCC/FDIC workflows), but filings explicitly call out AI as competitive threat, suggesting real (not just sympathy) risk. Basket rule applies (10-flag set). Defer new entry until thesis validation is sharper or existing positions establish recovery momentum.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Payroll is a regulated money-movement business (941s, state UI registrations, jurisdictional employment law) — Meta's WhatsApp customer-service agent has zero mechanism to handle this workflow. PAYC has its own real challenges (Beti adoption, sales execution) but they are unrelated to messaging-platform agents.
entries.what_kills: Acceleration of agentic back-office tools taking real PAYC client share — not credible in the next several quarters.
entries.reasoning: Threat=minimal, confidence=4 passes BUY eligibility. Thesis is sound (payroll is money-movement + tax compliance, not customer-service agent replacement). However, five-day move +4.17% suggests sympathy fade may be already partially repriced. Basket rule applies (10 flags). With 4 Codex positions open, adding PAYC creates concentration. WATCH for sharper breakdown in volume or a fresh catalyst.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Paylocity holds funds in transit for payroll, processes ACH transfers, and files multi-jurisdictional payroll taxes — none of which a WhatsApp Business customer-service agent addresses. Sympathy-fade behavior consistent with PAYC.
entries.what_kills: Same as PAYC — agentic back-office adoption far exceeding consensus.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (position opened same or prior session). Reconfirmed by this flag: threat=minimal, panic=unjustified, confidence=4. Identical thesis to PAYC (payroll + ACH moat). Existing position is the test; defer new entry to respect concentration guardrails.

entries.name: Workiva Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Regulatory-grade financial reporting and audit trail is the moat — AI can help draft, but the auditor-attested workflow and XBRL compliance is the product. WhatsApp customer-service agent has no overlap.
entries.what_kills: Big-4 auditors endorsing a horizontal AI-only workflow that bypasses Workiva — highly unlikely.
entries.reasoning: Threat=minimal, confidence=4 passes BUY eligibility. Thesis is sound (SEC filing/XBRL is regulatory moat, not AI-replaceable). However, five-day move +4.9% suggests partial recovery already priced. Basket rule applies (10-flag set). With 4 existing positions and high confidence in the Codex sympathy-fade read, defer new entries to avoid over-extension. Let existing positions generate price recovery data.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Government CIOs do not replace court systems of record with consumer AI agents. Tyler's procurement model (multi-year RFPs) and the regulatory environment make WhatsApp-style agentic replacement structurally implausible.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of major government bid losses to AI-native vendors — not credible at scale in the near term.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (position opened same or prior session). Reconfirmed by this flag: threat=none, panic=unjustified, confidence=4, five-day move +0.4%. Thesis intact (government procurement moat). Existing position is the test; do not re-enter.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
entries.thesis: Manhattan's WMS sits inside physical warehouse infrastructure with multi-year ERP integrations. OpenAI's six Codex verticals don't intersect with supply chain execution or WMS. The sympathy drop is purely thematic SaaS panic. Moat is customer switching cost on enterprise-grade physical operations, not text generation.
entries.what_kills: A major retail customer pilot of a competing AI-native WMS; cloud subscription deceleration tied to AI-driven price pressure.
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (position opened same or prior session). Reconfirmed by this flag: threat=minimal, panic=unjustified, confidence=4, five-day move +11.0%. Existing position is the test; do not re-enter. Filings confirm MANH is already embedding agentic AI into WMS, suggesting the sympathy fade may reverse sharply as market reprices.
